largest_rejected_p_note: BH is a step-up procedure that rejects every test up to the highest rank whose p-value clears its step-up bound. This is the LARGEST p-value among the rejected tests, not an alpha threshold; it can sit well below q when the p-values have a gap (the next-smallest p fails its own bound, so the step-up stops early).
family: exploratory only (Moran's I per gene + reproductive-rate quality test)
